title: Penn State Worry Questionnaire (PSWQ)
description: A questionnaire measuring the tendency to engage in excessive and uncontrollable worry.

instructions.title: Instructions
instructions.text: Indicate how typical each statement is of you.

options.label: Not at all typical of me

questions.text: If I do not have enough time to do everything, I do not worry about it.
I do not tend to worry about things.
I know I should not worry about things, but I just cannot help it.
When I am under pressure, I worry a lot.
I am always worrying about something.
I find it easy to dismiss worrisome thoughts.
As soon as I finish one task, I start to worry about everything else I have to do.
When there is nothing more I can do about a concern, I do not worry about it anymore.
I have been a worrier all my life.
I notice that I have been worrying about things.
Once I start worrying, I cannot stop.
I worry about projects until they are all done.

resultDescription: This questionnaire measures your general tendency to worry. Higher scores indicate a stronger tendency toward excessive and uncontrollable worry. This is not a diagnosis, but it can help you better understand your thinking patterns.
